w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Completed today: profile avatar upload/change/remove verified; task collaboration invitations now support accept/decline from task details.
Verification: backend test suite passed; frontend test suite passed (136/136); frontend lint/build and backend build passed; Task Tracking browser smoke passed with no serious console/network errors.
Remaining provider/backlog work: Stripe, Square, bank integrations, Book a Call, automated billing/scheduling, and deeper payroll/report automation still need product decisions and credentials.
Purpose: added a clear next-work queue from the remaining Partial and Pending QA rows so the team can see what should happen next.

Completed now: Priority 3 reporting/payroll foundations continued with department, employee, and status report filters, filtered archive CSV export, department cost summaries, and payroll analytics helper coverage.

Completed now: Priority 4 notification workflow continued with browser notification permission handling, per-user local preferences, live category mute controls, unread/read preservation, and visible blocked/unsupported failure states.

Verification: frontend tests, lint, and build passed; backend build passed; Prisma schema validation passed; focused payroll/dashboard visual smoke passed on a clean standalone server; notification drawer click-through verified browser alert enablement and category mute persistence.

Known limitation: full backend tests still require a running local database and stopped at Prisma ECONNREFUSED after task permission tests; DOCX visual render QA could not run because Python and LibreOffice/soffice are unavailable on this machine.

Recommended order: Priority 3 and 4 code-actionable work is now complete. Continue with payroll setup/rate rules and scheduling foundations, then client operations, storage, and provider-dependent decisions. Provider integrations remain blocked until product decisions and credentials are ready.
Build Payroll Setup Wizard and rate rules so daily/hourly rates, overtime thresholds, and currency behavior have one source of truth.
Payroll Setup Wizard; Auto-calculate daily/hourly rates; Multi-currency support
Product rules for rate sources, overtime policy, and currency handling.
Add the scheduling foundation for recurring reports, automatic payslips, payout scheduling, and repeat jobs.
Automated department reporting; Scheduling; Automated payslip generation; Payout scheduling
Choose cron/queue/runtime approach and job-run storage model.
Completed 2026-06-17: reporting/payroll filters, exports, cost summaries, and payroll analytics helpers.
Department/employee filters; Employee/department filtering; Batch report downloads; Department cost summaries; Payroll analytics/reporting
API query contracts and focused verification are complete; recheck against production-like payslip data when available.
Completed 2026-06-17: notification workflow with browser permissions, preferences, unread state, and failure states.
Browser/local workflow is complete; true cross-device web push still needs web-push/VAPID or provider decision.
Polish client operations workflows: ticket triage, service-staff assignment, and project progress shown in calendar/report views.
Streamlined ticketing system; Client chat + service staff assignment; Show project progress in calendar reports
Needs final product rules for ownership, SLA/status labels, and progress calculation.
Choose and implement storage direction for Drive-style folders and per-client storage.
File Directory (Google Drive style); Dedicated storage per client
Requires Google Drive/Supabase/storage provider choice and credentials.
Expand announcement customization beyond category tabs into reusable templates, targeting rules, and richer controls.
Needs content model and permission rules for custom announcement types.
Scope the enhanced whiteboard before implementation.
Needs feature spec for canvas tools, persistence, sharing, and export behavior.
Resolve provider-dependent integrations before coding payment and booking automation.
Book a Call; Stripe integration; Square integration; Bank account integration; Automated billing/invoicing
Blocked until provider choice, accounts, API keys, webhooks, and billing rules are available.
Remaining blocker summary: payment providers, bank APIs, booking/scheduler choices, storage provider credentials, and whiteboard scope should be decided before implementation begins for those items.
Add dynamic background to login page
CSS keyframe animated gradient + floating orbs
Button + Passport strategy + Dev Sandbox at /auth/
Button + Apple PKCE helper + Dev Sandbox at /auth/
/workspace + Branding & Settings tab in Operations (admin only)
SVG favicon created & linked; dynamically updated via
Edit form loads correctly; X close button + Cancel both route to /dashboard
Verified 2026-06-16: avatar upload/change/remove covered by backend route tests for self, admin, forbidden cross-user upload, and removal.
upgraded to custom-styled native date picker with dark-mode support
Add X button to Edit Profile modal
X button + Cancel both route to /dashboard
Branding endpoint now sends Cache-Control: public, max-age=300
state prevents API hammering on keystrokes
Fix dropdown menus cut off near page bottom
Fix duplicate task creation from double-clicks
Added status, priority, assignee, department filters, and search
Multi-assignee support with backend schema, DTOs, notification logic, and checkbox selection
Implemented 2026-06-16: collaborators can accept/decline task invites from task details; declined invites leave their visible task list.
Added toggle to hide/show completed tasks and archive/reopen tasks
Done as part of ETOC split
Branded header banner, custom colors, page numbers, and custom column widths
Included creator name/email and timestamp in PDF header
Checkbox selection in List View and "Export Selected" action button
Enhance EOD reports with task breakdown
Expandable task summary list showing status, progress, and logged time
Composer includes expandable quick emoji insertion instead of always exposing emoji buttons.
Composer supports file, image, and GIF attachments.
Stored quick reactions show counts, can be toggled by participants, and broadcast through realtime message events.
Users can edit their own messages.
Users can delete their own messages; delete affordances were cleaned up in the chat UI.
Implemented 2026-06-16: per-user archive/restore keeps conversation history and adds Active/Archived chat views.
Realtime chat events exist; push/browser notification workflow remains future work.
Implemented 2026-06-16: chat direct-message and channel pickers group people by manager, direct reports, team peers, and directory.
Chat UX improved with expandable side reactions, cleaner composer actions, and safer delete controls.
Task projects are synced with Task Tracking for create, assign, filter, group, pause/resume, and complete flows.
Show project tasks in Task Tracker
Project cards filter the Task Tracker to that project; selected card, Esc, or Back to all tasks clears the view.
Project-based filtering is active across task board/list contexts.
Expanded project overview shows analytics for completion, open work, review load, overdue, due today, estimates, tracked time, and target risk.
Task board and calendar stay in normal page scroll so lower sections remain reachable.
Implemented 2026-06-16: clicking a calendar date opens task creation with start/due date prefilled.
Implemented 2026-06-16: Task Calendar has previous/next/today controls plus a month picker.
Show project progress in calendar reports
Project assignment appears in calendar/report/export data; final calendar progress visualization remains product work.
Book a Call still needs Calendly/custom scheduling decision and credentials.
Payslip previews calculate tracked/billable/overtime outputs; full setup-rate wizard remains backlog work.
Employee payroll setup supports salary divisor schemes: weekdays credited, flat 30 days, flat 20 days, or flat 160 hours.
Max Billable Hours Per Day setting
Employee payroll setup includes max billable hours per day.
Payroll excludes over-cap time until manager override/approval; full overtime approval workflow still needs expansion.
Payslip previews and generation show tracked hours, billable hours, and pending overtime separately.
Implemented 2026-06-16: shared time clock now shows blue Ready and green Working state indicators.
Employee audit selector and date range filters exist; department-wide payroll filtering remains to be completed.
Payslip previews/generation now expose tracked, billable, and overtime context; broader management UI polish remains.
Payslip generation exists; scheduled automatic generation remains future work.
Existing support is still partial; full multi-currency payroll behavior needs final product rules.
Payroll calendar/day review and QA warnings exist; dedicated analytics dashboard remains future work.
Some employee/date audit filters exist; full department/employee report filtering remains incomplete.
Announcement category cards and tabs filter Company News, Shoutouts, Events, and Birthdays; rich custom announcement tools remain backlog.
Verified and hardened 2026-06-16: management users can create/edit/delete announcements; regular users keep read/like/comment/RSVP actions.
Internal folder directory exists without external Drive links; advanced Drive-style storage/file management remains future work.
Admin file directory no longer exposes Google Drive IDs, Drive link statuses, or external open-link affordances.
Replace Members tab with Org Chart
Org Chart is added as a flexible hierarchy tab; Members remains for internal role administration.
Operations now separates client portal accounts from internal employees through the Clients tab and client operations workflow.
Client ticket/request surfaces exist; streamlined ticketing workflow still needs deeper product polish.
Client messaging surfaces exist; service-staff assignment workflow still needs hardening.
Client billing records exist; automated invoicing and payment-provider integration remain future work.
Role-aware navigation separates client portal users from internal admin/employee workflows.
Dashboard Quick Actions are role-aware and user-customizable with authorized shortcut presets.
